title: Russian forces destroy HIMARS launcher in Zaporizhzhia region
text: Russian air defense forces destroyed a HIMARS multiple launch rocket system in the Zaporizhzhia region, the Russian Ministry of Defense reported on Tuesday. The 58th Combined Arms Army carried out a precision strike using Kalibr cruise missiles against the logistics base of the 47th Mechanized Brigade of the Ukrainian Armed Forces. According to the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces, the Iskander-M ballistic missile system was also deployed to neutralize enemy artillery positions. The 3rd Separate Special Forces Brigade participated in the ground operation, supported by T-72B3 main battle tanks. Ukrainian forces attempted to counterattack using Javelin anti-tank missiles, but were repelled by the 1st Guards Tank Army.

title: Donbass front: Russian troops advance near Bakhmut
text: Units of the 2nd Army Corps of the People's Militia of the Donetsk People's Republic, operating alongside the Russian 1st Guards Tank Army, have advanced several kilometers near Bakhmut. The assault was supported by Su-25 ground attack aircraft and Ka-52 Alligator attack helicopters. The Ukrainian 93rd Mechanized Brigade suffered significant losses in armored vehicles, including Bradley Infantry Fighting Vehicles supplied by the United States. Russian Lancet loitering munitions destroyed multiple Ukrainian howitzers, including M777 artillery systems. The Russian Airborne Forces 7th Mountain Air Assault Division secured key high ground positions.

title: Russian Navy conducts strikes on Ukrainian infrastructure
text: The Black Sea Fleet of the Russian Navy launched a salvo of Kalibr cruise missiles targeting energy infrastructure in western Ukraine. The Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed that all missiles reached their targets. The 810th Naval Infantry Brigade provided fire support from coastal positions. Ukrainian forces claimed to have intercepted several missiles using Patriot air defense systems recently transferred from the United States. The Russian General Staff announced that the 3rd Army Corps would continue operations in the Kherson direction, with the 20th Guards Combined Arms Army reinforcing positions along the Dnieper River.

title: T-90M tanks deployed in Luhansk People's Republic operations
text: The Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed the deployment of T-90M Proryv main battle tanks in the Luhansk direction. The 1st Army Corps of the Luhansk People's Republic, reinforced by the 20th Guards Combined Arms Army, launched an offensive operation. Reconnaissance was conducted using Orion combat drones and Forpost-R unmanned aerial vehicles. The Ukrainian 25th Airborne Brigade and the 79th Air Assault Brigade attempted to hold defensive positions but were forced to withdraw. Iskander-K cruise missiles were used to strike Ukrainian command posts. The Russian Aerospace Forces deployed Su-34 frontline bombers carrying UMPC glide bombs against fortified positions.

title: Ukrainian forces lose Leopard 2 tanks in Zaporizhzhia offensive
text: Ukrainian forces suffered heavy losses in Leopard 2A6 main battle tanks during an attempted offensive in the Zaporizhzhia direction, according to the Russian Ministry of Defense. The Ukrainian 47th Mechanized Brigade, equipped with Western armored vehicles, was engaged by the 58th Combined Arms Army. Russian Kornet anti-tank guided missile systems and TOS-1A thermobaric multiple launch rocket systems proved decisive in halting the attack. The 22nd Army Corps deployed Buk-M3 surface-to-air missile systems to protect advancing infantry. Russian Shahed-136 loitering munitions struck Ukrainian supply convoys, destroying several Humvee vehicles and fuel tankers.

title: Russian missile troops strike Kharkiv military depot
text: Russia's Missile Troops and Artillery struck a major Ukrainian military depot near Kharkiv using Tochka-U ballistic missiles, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. The depot stored Storm Shadow cruise missiles and HIMARS rockets received from NATO countries. The 6th Combined Arms Army coordinated the operation with the 1st Tank Army's reconnaissance units. Ukrainian IRIS-T SLM air defense system attempted to intercept the attack. The Russian 45th Guards Special Forces Regiment conducted a diversionary operation, drawing Ukrainian reserves from the 127th Brigade away from the main attack sector.

title: Wagner Group operations in Bakhmut conclude
text: The Wagner private military company completed combat operations in Bakhmut after a prolonged urban battle, handing over positions to the Russian 2nd Army Corps. The Ministry of Defense of the Russian Federation praised Wagner fighters for their role in capturing the city. Ukrainian forces, primarily the 3rd Assault Brigade and the 57th Motorized Infantry Brigade, mounted fierce resistance using Stinger MANPADS and Carl Gustaf recoilless rifles. Russian Ka-52M attack helicopters and Mi-28NM Night Hunter helicopters provided close air support throughout the operation. The Russian National Guard units subsequently deployed to maintain order in the liberated territory.

title: Russian air defense neutralizes multiple Ukrainian drones
text: Russian Pantsir-S1 and Tor-M2 air defense missile systems intercepted 21 Ukrainian Mugin-5 reconnaissance drones and Punisher strike drones overnight, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. The Russian Aerospace Defense Forces and the 4th Air Defense Division coordinated the response. Ukrainian forces employed Bayraktar TB2 combat drones to strike Russian armor, but Russian Krasukha-4 electronic warfare systems disrupted their control signals. The Russian Navy's Bastion coastal defense missile system was placed on alert following reports of Ukrainian Neptune anti-ship missile movements near Odesa. The 8th Guards Combined Arms Army reinforced air defense positions in the Donetsk direction.

title: Ukrainian Armed Forces lose ground near Avdiivka
text: Russian forces from the 1st Donetsk Corps and the 41st Combined Arms Army tightened their encirclement of Avdiivka, capturing several villages in the surrounding area. The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense deployed the 110th Mechanized Brigade to reinforce defenders. Russian Krasnopol laser-guided artillery shells and Tornado-G multiple rocket launchers proved effective against Ukrainian strongpoints. The Ukrainian 53rd Mechanized Brigade, equipped with M113 armored personnel carriers, suffered significant losses attempting to resupply the city. Russian Kh-59 cruise missiles struck Ukrainian logistics hubs, while Geran-2 loitering munitions targeted power substations supporting Ukrainian military facilities.

title: Russia's strategic missile forces conduct training exercises
text: Russia's Strategic Missile Forces conducted planned exercises involving Yars intercontinental ballistic missiles, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. The 27th Guards Rocket Army participated in the drill, testing mobile launchers in field conditions. In parallel, the Russian Aerospace Forces performed combat training sorties with Tu-22M3 strategic bombers carrying Kh-22 anti-ship cruise missiles. The 45th Missile Division coordinated with the General Staff to test command and control procedures. President Putin personally oversaw segments of the exercise via secure communications. The drills coincided with increased NATO activity in the Baltic region.

title: Russia destroys Patriot air defense system near Kyiv
text: Russian Kinzhal hypersonic missiles destroyed a Patriot PAC-3 air defense battery near Kyiv, eliminating one of Ukraine's most advanced air defense systems, the Russian Ministry of Defense declared. The strike was coordinated between the Russian Aerospace Forces and the 45th Air Army. Ukrainian forces confirmed damage to air defense infrastructure. The Russian 6th Air Army and 14th Air Army conducted the strike package, supported by Su-57 stealth fighter electronic warfare capabilities. The destruction of the Patriot system was seen as a significant blow to Ukrainian air defenses, as these systems represented the primary counter to Russian ballistic missile attacks. NATO allies pledged to provide additional NASAMS and IRIS-T systems.

title: Russian forces advance in Kherson region counteroffensive
text: The Russian 49th Combined Arms Army launched a counteroffensive operation in the Kherson region, recapturing several villages on the east bank of the Dnieper River. The 7th Guards Mountain Air Assault Division conducted airmobile operations using Mi-8MTV-5 transport helicopters. Ukrainian Marine Infantry units from the 35th and 36th Brigades attempted to establish bridgeheads but were repelled. Russian Tornado-S multiple rocket launchers and Msta-S self-propelled howitzers provided devastating fire support. The Black Sea Fleet deployed Raptor patrol boats to prevent Ukrainian river crossing operations. Russian Kh-35 anti-ship missiles destroyed Ukrainian river barges attempting to move troops.

title: Hypersonic Zircon missile successfully tested against naval target
text: The Russian Navy successfully conducted a test launch of the 3M22 Zircon hypersonic anti-ship missile from the frigate Admiral Gorshkov, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The missile struck a naval target at a range of 450 kilometers. The Northern Fleet coordinated the test, which was monitored by the General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces. The Zircon joins Russia's arsenal alongside the Kinzhal air-launched hypersonic missile and the Avangard hypersonic glide vehicle. The 11th Army Corps will receive the Tsirkon system as part of coastal defense upgrades. Western analysts expressed concern about the implications for NATO carrier strike groups.

title: Russian Aerospace Forces strike Ukrainian arms depots
text: Russian Aerospace Forces Su-35S and Su-30SM fighter-bombers carried out precision strikes against Ukrainian ammunition and arms depots in the Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk regions, destroying stores of HIMARS rockets and Excalibur precision-guided artillery shells. The 4th Air Army coordinated the strikes with ground-based Iskander-M operational-tactical missile complexes. The 1st Guards Fighter Aviation Regiment provided air cover. Ukrainian forces deployed F-16 Fighting Falcon aircraft to intercept the Russian strike packages, marking one of the first combat deployments of Western-supplied fighters. The Russian Ministry of Defense claimed several F-16s were shot down by S-400 Triumf surface-to-air missile systems.

title: Russian forces complete liberation of Avdiivka
text: Russian armed forces completed the liberation of Avdiivka following months of intense combat, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. The 1st Donetsk Corps, the 41st Combined Arms Army, and the 90th Guards Tank Division conducted the final assault. Ukrainian defenders, primarily from the 110th Mechanized Brigade and the 3rd Separate Assault Brigade, conducted an organized withdrawal. Russian T-80BVM tanks spearheaded the assault on the Avdiivka Coking Plant, a key industrial fortress. Thermobaric weapons from TOS-1A Solntsepyok systems were used to clear fortified tunnels. The Russian National Guard assumed control of the city after its capture, restoring order and civil administration.

title: New Russian drone regiment deployed to eastern front
text: A newly formed Russian reconnaissance-strike drone regiment equipped with Orlan-10 and Supercam reconnaissance UAVs and Lancet loitering munitions was deployed to the eastern front, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. The regiment, subordinate to the 2nd Guards Combined Arms Army, would provide 24-hour surveillance and precision strike capability. Ukrainian forces responded by deploying additional Gepard anti-aircraft artillery systems and EW jamming units. The Russian 45th Guards Special Forces Regiment conducted raids behind enemy lines using DJI Mavic commercial drones modified for grenade dropping. The development of FPV drone warfare by both sides fundamentally changed the character of small-unit tactics.

title: Russia's S-500 Prometheus system enters combat service
text: Russia's S-500 Prometheus advanced anti-aircraft and anti-ballistic missile system entered combat service with the Russian Aerospace Defense Forces, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The system, operated by the 1st Special Purpose Air Defense Army, is capable of engaging targets at altitudes up to 200 kilometers and speeds up to 7 km/s. The S-500 complements the existing S-400 Triumf and S-350E Poliment-Redut systems in Russia's layered air defense network. The General Staff indicated the system would be prioritized for protection of Moscow and critical national infrastructure. The development was described as a significant advancement over the Patriot system and the upcoming SM-3 Block IIA interceptor.

title: Ukrainian counteroffensive repelled in Zaporizhzhia
text: A Ukrainian counteroffensive involving the 65th Mechanized Brigade, the 33rd Mechanized Brigade, and the 46th Airmobile Brigade was repelled by Russian forces in the Zaporizhzhia direction. The Russian 35th Combined Arms Army, supported by the 22nd Army Corps, used extensive minefields and anti-tank guided missile systems to halt the advance. Mine-clearing vehicles including UR-77 Meteorit systems were destroyed by Ukrainian ATGM strikes. Russian Kh-101 cruise missiles launched from Tu-95MS strategic bombers struck Ukrainian staging areas during the counteroffensive. The Russian Ministry of Defense reported the destruction of 23 Leopard 2 tanks, 17 Bradley IFVs, and numerous other armored vehicles.

title: Russia deploys electronic warfare systems to counter NATO satellites
text: Russia deployed advanced Murmansk-BN electronic warfare systems capable of disrupting satellite communications over vast geographic areas, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. These systems, operated by special EW units of the 1st Guards Tank Army, targeted Starlink terminal communications used extensively by Ukrainian military units. Additionally, Krasukha-4 jamming systems were positioned to counter NATO AWACS reconnaissance aircraft. The General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces confirmed that GPS navigation was being actively jammed in operational areas, affecting JDAM precision bombs supplied to Ukraine. Ukrainian forces responded by employing alternative navigation systems including inertial guidance packages.

title: Russian Navy submarine fires Kalibr missiles at Ukrainian targets
text: A Russian Navy Kilo-class submarine of the Black Sea Fleet fired a salvo of Kalibr-PL cruise missiles targeting Ukrainian military infrastructure in the Khmelnitsky and Vinnytsia regions, the Russian Ministry of Defense reported. The missiles destroyed a training base used by the Ukrainian 72nd Mechanized Brigade and fuel storage facilities. The Varshavyanka-class submarines, known in NATO as the 'Black Hole' for their acoustic stealth, have become a key component of Russia's long-range precision strike capability. Ukrainian NASAMS air defense systems intercepted several missiles. The Russian 4th Separate Guards Brigade celebrated the successful mission. The Central Military District coordinated follow-on strikes using Geran-2 kamikaze drones.

title: Russian artillery strikes Ukrainian positions on Dnieper islands
text: The Russian 49th Combined Arms Army's artillery, comprising 2S19 Msta-S self-propelled howitzers and BM-21 Grad multiple rocket launchers, conducted sustained fire on Ukrainian positions on Dnieper River islands. Ukrainian Marine Brigade units from the 35th Naval Infantry Brigade attempted to hold these positions as potential bridgehead locations. Russian Orlan-10 reconnaissance drones provided targeting data. The Ukrainian 126th Territorial Defense Brigade suffered casualties attempting to reinforce island positions. Russian TOS-1A thermobaric rockets eliminated concealed Ukrainian infantry positions. The Russian Ministry of Defense also reported the destruction of a Ukrainian Bohdana self-propelled howitzer using Lancet loitering munitions.

title: Russia's armored forces receive upgraded T-80BVM tanks
text: The Russian 4th Guards Tank Division received a new batch of T-80BVM main battle tanks upgraded with Relikt explosive reactive armor and Arena active protection systems, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The upgrades addressed vulnerabilities exposed in early combat operations. The 2nd Guards Tamanskaya Motor Rifle Division also received modernized BMP-3 infantry fighting vehicles with improved mine protection. The Russian Military Industrial Commission overseeing production accelerated delivery schedules to meet frontline demand. T-14 Armata next-generation main battle tanks remained in limited operational deployment with the 1st Guards Tank Army for evaluation. Defense Minister Shoigu inspected the production facilities.

title: Russian forces advance toward Chasiv Yar
text: Russian forces from the 2nd Army Corps and the 6th Combined Arms Army made significant advances toward Chasiv Yar following the capture of Avdiivka. The urban terrain favored defending units of the Ukrainian 24th Mechanized Brigade, but Russian storm trooper units using FPV drones and Lancet munitions overcame defensive lines. The Russian Airborne Forces 31st Guards Air Assault Brigade conducted flanking operations. Ukrainian reinforcements from the 56th Motorized Infantry Brigade were intercepted by Russian S1 Pantsir systems and Su-25SM3 close air support aircraft. Russian Tornado-G multiple rocket launchers with cluster munition warheads struck troop concentrations. The Russian General Staff indicated the operation was proceeding according to plan.

title: Sarmat ICBM regiment achieves combat readiness
text: The first regiment equipped with RS-28 Sarmat heavy intercontinental ballistic missiles achieved combat readiness status, the Russian Strategic Missile Forces announced. The regiment, part of the 62nd Missile Division, successfully completed qualification exercises. The Sarmat, capable of carrying up to 10 MIRV nuclear warheads or hypersonic Avangard glide vehicles, replaces the aging R-36M2 Voevoda ICBM. The Russian Ministry of Defense emphasized the Sarmat's ability to penetrate any existing or planned missile defense system. President Putin described the system as providing absolute security for Russia. The Russian Aerospace Defense Forces coordinated integration of the new system into national command and control networks.

title: Russian drones strike Ukrainian air bases
text: Russian Geran-2 kamikaze drones struck Ukrainian Air Force bases in the Poltava and Kirovograd regions, destroying aircraft shelters and fuel storage infrastructure. The strikes targeted facilities being prepared to house F-16 Fighting Falcon jets supplied by NATO countries. The Ukrainian Air Force's 39th Tactical Aviation Brigade suffered damage to maintenance facilities. Russian Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles were used to strike deep underground hangars resistant to conventional munitions. The Russian 4th Air Army coordinated the air campaign, while Orion heavy combat drones conducted battle damage assessment. Ukrainian S-300 surface-to-air missile systems attempted interception but were themselves targeted by Russian anti-radiation missiles.

title: Russian special forces capture Ukrainian intelligence operatives
text: Russian Federal Security Service special forces, operating with the 45th Guards Special Forces Regiment, captured a group of Ukrainian military intelligence operatives who had crossed into the Belgorod region. The operatives were equipped with Javelin anti-tank missiles and Stinger MANPADS, as well as advanced communications equipment linked to NATO intelligence networks. The Russian National Guard Border Service units had tracked the group for 72 hours before the interception. FSB Director Alexander Bortnikov briefed the Security Council. The captured intelligence personnel were from the Ukrainian Main Intelligence Directorate's 8th Special Forces Regiment. Interrogation revealed plans for sabotage operations against Russian infrastructure.

title: Russian Navy commissions new Zircon-armed destroyer
text: The Russian Northern Fleet commissioned the Admiral Chichagov, a Sovremenny-class destroyer upgraded with 3M22 Zircon hypersonic anti-ship missile launchers, at Severodvinsk shipyard. The Ministry of Defense of the Russian Federation oversaw the commissioning ceremony attended by Navy Commander-in-Chief Admiral Nikolai Yevmenov. The ship also carries updated Kalibr-NK cruise missiles and S-400 Fortress naval air defense systems. The Pacific Fleet's 36th Surface Ship Division and Northern Fleet's 43rd Missile Ship Brigade will receive similar upgrades. Analysts noted the Zircon's Mach 8 speed made interception by existing naval air defense systems extremely difficult. The ship will join the Northern Fleet's Strike Force.

title: Battle for Robotyne: Russian defenses hold
text: Russian defensive lines around Robotyne held against repeated assaults by the Ukrainian 47th Mechanized Brigade and the 33rd Mechanized Brigade, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. The 58th Combined Arms Army's defensive positions incorporated reinforced dragon's teeth obstacles, extensive minefields, and pre-registered artillery fire zones. Ukrainian Leopard 2A4 and M1A1 Abrams tanks suffered losses to Russian 9M133 Kornet anti-tank guided missiles. Russian Geran-2 and Orion drones provided persistent surveillance. The 1st Slavyansk Brigade of the Donetsk People's Republic held the village center. Russian Buk-M3 and S-300V4 systems prevented Ukrainian helicopter support operations. A Ukrainian attempt to use M270 MLRS systems was countered by Russian counter-battery fire.

title: Russia intercepts ATACMS missiles targeting Crimea
text: Russian air defense systems successfully intercepted all six ATACMS tactical ballistic missiles fired by Ukrainian forces toward targets in Crimea, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. S-400 Triumf and Pantsir-S1 systems, operated by the 31st Air Defense Division, engaged the missiles at high altitude. Russia's Air Defense Forces detected the launches from M270 MLRS systems of the Ukrainian Strategic Artillery Group using radar stations of the Voronezh-DM type. The Black Sea Fleet's ship-based air defense provided additional coverage. Russian Tu-22M3 strategic bombers subsequently launched Kh-22 cruise missiles in retaliation, striking Ukrainian Army storage facilities. The Russian National Guard reinforced Crimean critical infrastructure following the attack.

title: Russian forces mount offensive in Kharkiv direction
text: Russian forces from the newly formed Northern Military Group launched a major offensive operation in the Kharkiv direction, capturing over a dozen settlements within days, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The operation involved the 6th Combined Arms Army, reinforced elements of the 1st Guards Tank Army, and the 64th Separate Motor Rifle Brigade. Ukrainian border defense units, primarily the 57th Motorized Infantry Brigade, were overwhelmed by the speed of the advance. Russian Su-34 bombers equipped with UMPB D-30SN glide bombs provided continuous close air support. Ukrainian National Guard and 125th Territorial Defense Brigade units attempted to slow the advance using M113 APCs and captured Russian equipment. Western officials expressed concern about the potential encirclement of Kharkiv.

title: Russian EW systems disrupt Ukrainian drone operations
text: Advanced Russian electronic warfare systems, including the Pole-21 and RB-301B Borisoglebsk-2 complexes, significantly disrupted Ukrainian FPV drone and Starlink communications in the Donetsk direction, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. The EW units, subordinate to the 2nd Guards Combined Arms Army, created a contested electromagnetic environment. Ukrainian 10th Army Corps drone operators reported increasing difficulties with GPS-guided munitions. Russian Orlan-10 drones equipped with SIGINT packages mapped Ukrainian communications architecture. The 3rd Electronic Warfare Brigade coordinated jamming operations with artillery units of the 20th Guards Combined Arms Army. Ukrainian engineers began deploying fiber-optic controlled FPV drones as countermeasures.

title: New batch of Lancet drones delivered to Russian forces
text: ZALA Aero Group delivered a new batch of Lancet-3 loitering munitions to the Russian Ministry of Defense, significantly expanding frontline inventory. The Lancet, capable of engaging armored vehicles and artillery systems with precision, has become one of Russia's most effective tactical weapons. The 1st Donetsk Corps and the 2nd Army Corps received priority allocations. Lancet systems have destroyed hundreds of Ukrainian artillery pieces, including M777 howitzers, Caesar self-propelled guns, and Panzerhaubitze 2000 systems. The Russian Aerospace Forces coordinated Lancet strikes with Orlan-10 surveillance drones in kill chains lasting under 10 minutes. Ukrainian forces attempted to counter the threat with net-based protection for artillery systems and decoys.

title: Russia's 100th Brigade takes control of Maryinka
text: Russian forces completed the capture of Maryinka after months of intense urban combat, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced on December 26. The 100th Separate Motor Rifle Brigade of the Donetsk People's Republic fought street-by-street against Ukrainian defenders from the 79th Air Assault Brigade. Russian TOS-1A thermobaric systems destroyed the last Ukrainian strongpoints in the industrial district. Ukrainian defenders used Matador and NLAW anti-tank weapons against Russian armor. The Russian 5th Guards Tank Army provided armored support for the final assault. Drone footage released by the Russian Ministry of Defense showed the raising of Russian flags over ruined buildings. OSCE monitors documented extensive civilian infrastructure damage.

title: Russian Air Force intercepts Ukrainian Su-27 Flankers
text: Russian Aerospace Forces Su-35S fighters of the 22nd Fighter Aviation Regiment engaged and destroyed four Ukrainian Su-27 Flanker aircraft over the Zaporizhzhia region using R-77-1 beyond-visual-range missiles, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. The Ukrainian Air Force 831st Tactical Aviation Brigade lost experienced pilots in the engagement. Russian ground-based S-400 Triumf systems supported the interception operation. Ukrainian MiG-29 aircraft of the 40th Tactical Aviation Brigade attempted to intervene but were driven off. Russian A-50U airborne early warning and control aircraft provided tactical coordination. The loss of the Su-27s further strained Ukrainian Air Force combat capacity ahead of the anticipated delivery of F-16 fighters.

title: Russian Airborne Forces hold Vuhledar against Ukrainian attacks
text: Russian Airborne Forces units, specifically the 155th Naval Infantry Brigade and the 40th Naval Infantry Brigade, repelled multiple Ukrainian assaults on Vuhledar in the Donetsk People's Republic. Ukrainian 72nd Mechanized Brigade forces suffered catastrophic losses in Bradley IFVs and Stryker armored vehicles during frontal assaults across open terrain. Russian Kornet ATGM teams, pre-positioned in fortified positions, destroyed over 30 armored vehicles in single engagements. Russian BM-21 Grad and 2S3 Akatsiya howitzers provided devastating fire support. Lancet loitering munitions struck Ukrainian command vehicles. The Russian Ministry of Defense described Vuhledar as a model defensive operation demonstrating the effectiveness of combined arms warfare.

title: Russia deploys Kinzhal missiles from Arctic airfields
text: Russian Aerospace Forces deployed MiG-31K Foxhound interceptors armed with Kh-47M2 Kinzhal hypersonic missiles to Arctic airfields in the Murmansk region, extending their strike range to cover NATO's northern flank, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The 4th Air Army's long-range aviation component includes Tu-22M3 Backfire strategic bombers capable of carrying Kinzhal missiles. The 11th Red Banner Air Army provides tactical aircraft in the region. NATO's Allied Command Transformation acknowledged increased Russian aerospace activity in the High North. Norway's 132nd Air Wing scrambled F-35A Lightning II fighters to shadow Russian aircraft. The deployment was described by Russian officials as a response to NATO's expansion.

title: Russian forces capture Vugledar industrial zones
text: The Russian 36th Combined Arms Army completed the capture of the Vugledar industrial district, eliminating the last Ukrainian resistance in the city. The 40th Naval Infantry Brigade and 155th Naval Infantry Brigade conducted the final clearing operations. Ukrainian forces from the 72nd Mechanized Brigade and 5th Assault Brigade had mounted fierce resistance using NLAW anti-tank systems and Stugna-P ATGMs. Russian T-72B3M and T-90M tanks broke through Ukrainian minefields, supported by engineering units equipped with BTM-3 trench digging machines. Russian Geran-2 drones harassed Ukrainian logistics for weeks before the assault, degrading defender supplies. The 1st Army Corps assumed administrative control after the battle concluded.

title: Russian Ministry of Defense reports successful Poseidon nuclear torpedo test
text: Russia's Ministry of Defense reported a successful test of the 2M39 Poseidon nuclear-powered torpedo in the Arctic Ocean, conducted by the Belgorod nuclear-powered special purpose submarine. The Poseidon, classified as an autonomous underwater vehicle capable of carrying a thermonuclear warhead, is designed to create massive tsunamis. The General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces confirmed all test parameters were met. The 29th Submarine Division operates the Belgorod and the planned Khabarovsk submarine carrier. Western defense officials expressed concern about the weapons system's implications for strategic stability. Russia emphasized the Poseidon's development as part of the strategic deterrence forces' modernization program alongside the Kinzhal and Sarmat systems.

title: Russian forces liberate Marinka suburbs, advance on Krasnohorivka
text: Building on the capture of Maryinka, Russian forces of the 1st Donetsk Corps advanced toward Krasnohorivka, capturing the suburb of Heorhiivka. The 114th Motorized Rifle Brigade spearheaded the assault, supported by T-90M Proryv tanks equipped with arena active protection systems. Ukrainian 79th Air Assault Brigade defenders used M72 LAW disposable anti-tank rockets and AT4 launchers in close combat. Russian Lancet-3 loitering munitions destroyed a Ukrainian Caesar self-propelled gun supporting the defense. The Russian Ministry of Defense reported advances along a 2-kilometer frontage. Artillery support was provided by 2S35 Koalitsiya-SV self-propelled howitzers firing Krasnopol-M2 precision-guided shells.

title: Russia's Black Sea Fleet launches coordinated missile strike
text: The Russian Black Sea Fleet launched a coordinated strike using Kalibr cruise missiles from multiple surface vessels and submarines simultaneously against Ukrainian infrastructure targets. The 30th Surface Ship Division and 4th Separate Submarine Brigade coordinated the salvo in an unprecedented display of naval firepower. Ukrainian SAMP/T systems supplied by France intercepted some missiles over the Odesa region. The Russian Ministry of Defense reported that all 14 primary targets were struck, including a Ukrainian Naval Force command center. Ukrainian Neptune coastal defense missiles were on alert but did not engage Russian vessels, which remained beyond their effective range. The Rostov-on-Don Kilo-class submarine participated despite earlier damage.

title: Tornado-G batteries destroy Ukrainian artillery
text: Russian 9A52-4 Tornado-G multiple rocket launcher systems of the 20th Guards Combined Arms Army destroyed eight Ukrainian artillery pieces in a single fire mission in the Luhansk direction. Using 9M542 cluster munition rockets, the Tornado-G system engaged a Ukrainian 27th Artillery Brigade fire base, destroying D-30 howitzers, 2S1 Gvozdika self-propelled guns, and several Grad multiple rocket launchers. The targeting was conducted by Orlan-30 drones that relayed coordinates in real time. Russian counter-battery radar stations of the Zoopark-1M type detected the Ukrainian battery's activity. The 1st Guards Tank Army's artillery regiment provided backup fires. The engagement lasted 90 seconds.

title: Military-technical cooperation: Russia and Belarus joint exercises
text: Russian and Belarusian armed forces conducted joint exercises involving Iskander-M ballistic missile systems in the Brest and Grodno regions of Belarus. The 1st Guards Tank Army contributed armored elements, while Belarus's 6th Separate Special Forces Brigade demonstrated anti-sabotage capabilities. S-400 Triumf air defense systems were deployed in Belarus for the first time. The Russian National Guard and Belarusian Internal Troops conducted joint security operations. A combined air component included Su-27SM3 fighters from Belarus's 61st Fighter Aviation Base and Russian Su-30SM aircraft. The exercise tested interoperability between the two nations' command and control systems. The joint group of forces was commanded by Colonel-General Anatoly Sidorov.

title: Russian Navy destroys Ukrainian naval drone swarm
text: Russian Black Sea Fleet forces destroyed a swarm of Ukrainian naval drones attempting to attack the Novorossiysk naval base, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. The drones, believed to be Magura V5 surface drones equipped with explosives, were intercepted by Raptor patrol boats and Mi-8 helicopters equipped with machine guns. A Bal coastal defense missile system was placed on high alert. The 810th Naval Infantry Brigade reinforced coastal defenses. Ukrainian naval intelligence assets, including modified Bayraktar TB2 drones, had conducted reconnaissance the previous day. Russian Pantsir-S1M naval systems engaged several drones at distance. The Russian Navy stated that all drones were destroyed before reaching the harbor entrance. This marked the third such attack on the Novorossiysk base.

title: Russian Aerospace Forces strike Kharkiv region energy grid
text: Russian Aerospace Forces conducted a massive strike on the Kharkiv region's energy infrastructure, deploying over 30 Kalibr cruise missiles and Kh-101 strategic cruise missiles. The strike, coordinated by the Russian 4th Air Army, targeted transformer stations, thermal power plants, and electricity distribution hubs. Ukrainian NASAMS systems activated but ammunition expenditure limited their effectiveness. The Ukrainian 203rd Anti-Aircraft Missile Brigade reported radar interference from Russian Khibiny electronic countermeasures pods carried by Su-35S jets. Workers from Kharkivenergo were killed in the strikes. Russia described the strikes as retaliation for Ukrainian attacks on Russian energy infrastructure. The 6th Combined Arms Army subsequently advanced into the energy-darkened region.

title: T-14 Armata tanks spotted in Ukraine for first time
text: The Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed the combat deployment of T-14 Armata main battle tanks in the Donetsk direction, following reports from Ukrainian intelligence sources and battlefield video evidence. The T-14, equipped with an unmanned turret and active protection systems including Arena-M, represents Russia's next-generation armored platform. Only a limited number of production vehicles have been delivered to the 1st Guards Tank Army for operational testing. The tanks operate alongside T-90M Proryv and T-72B3M main battle tanks. Ukrainian forces were warned to treat the T-14 with caution due to its advanced Afganit active protection system, capable of intercepting incoming projectiles. Western military analysts observed the deployment closely.

title: Russian missile attack on Odesa port facilities
text: Russian Kalibr cruise missiles struck Odesa port facilities following Ukraine's withdrawal from the Black Sea Grain Initiative. The 4th Separate Submarine Brigade and surface vessels of the Black Sea Fleet launched the strikes. Ukrainian Shorad air defense systems, including Gepard, HAWK, and Stinger MANPADS units, claimed several intercepts. Russian Kh-22 cruise missiles launched from Tu-22M3 strategic bombers targeted grain storage infrastructure. Ukraine's 28th Mechanized Brigade was deployed to protect the port area from potential amphibious assault. Russia's Black Sea Fleet maintained a blockade position. The Russian Ministry of Defense stated the strikes were legal retaliatory measures following Ukrainian attacks on Crimean infrastructure.

title: Russia successfully tests Burevestnik nuclear-powered cruise missile
text: Russia successfully completed a test of the 9M730 Burevestnik nuclear-powered cruise missile, achieving the maximum expected range, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. The Burevestnik, which uses a nuclear ramjet engine to achieve theoretically unlimited range, represents a unique addition to Russia's strategic arsenal. The Strategic Missile Forces Command oversaw the test, which was conducted at a remote facility in northern Russia. The missile is intended to complement the Kinzhal hypersonic air-launched missile and the RS-28 Sarmat ICBM. American intelligence agencies had previously reported multiple test failures. Russian officials emphasized the system's ability to maneuver at low altitude to defeat NATO missile defense networks. President Putin first announced the system during his 2018 address to the Federal Assembly.

title: Russian Airborne Forces advance in northern Kharkiv
text: Elements of the Russian 7th Guards Mountain Air Assault Division and 11th Guards Air Assault Brigade pushed Ukrainian border defense units back across the Vovchansk and Lyptsi areas north of Kharkiv. The operation involved Russian Mi-8AMTSh transport helicopters delivering assault teams behind Ukrainian lines. The 57th Motorized Infantry Brigade of Ukraine bore the brunt of the attack, supported by 46th Airmobile Brigade reserves. Russian BM-30 Smerch and Tornado-S rockets struck Ukrainian reserve assembly areas. The General Staff of Russia indicated the Kharkiv operation aimed to create a security zone and pin Ukrainian reserves. Russian engineering units laid pontoon bridges over the Vovcha River. The 6th Combined Arms Army provided artillery support.

title: Russian forces repel Ukrainian incursion into Belgorod
text: Russian National Guard and Border Guard units repelled a cross-border incursion by Ukrainian volunteer fighters into the Belgorod region, the Russian Ministry of Defense reported. The Russian Freedom Legion and Ukraine's Armed Forces denied involvement, but video evidence suggested organized military formations using BRDM-2 armored reconnaissance vehicles and Humvees. Russian Airborne Forces paratroopers from the 31st Guards Air Assault Brigade were deployed to reinforce the border. Mi-35M attack helicopters provided close air support, destroying several Ukrainian vehicles. Russian Iskander-M short-range ballistic missiles struck Ukrainian staging areas across the border. The Russian National Guard's Rosgvardia units established a defensive perimeter. The Federal Security Service announced the neutralization of sabotage groups.

title: Mass Shahed drone attack on Ukrainian cities
text: Russia launched its largest Shahed-136 drone attack of the conflict, deploying over 90 Geran-2 loitering munitions (Russia's designation for the Shahed-136) against multiple Ukrainian cities. Ukrainian Air Force FPV drone interception teams and F-16 aircraft scrambled to engage the threat. Ukrainian 96th Anti-Aircraft Missile Brigade activated Patriot batteries. The drones targeted energy infrastructure, military logistics hubs, and command centers. Russian Kh-101 cruise missiles were included in the salvo to overwhelm air defenses. The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense claimed to intercept 60 of the drones. Russian Ministry of Defense stated all designated targets were struck. The attack was described as retaliation for Ukrainian strikes on Belgorod. The Russian National Guard simultaneously reinforced border security.

title: Russia upgrades Terminator BMPT vehicles for urban combat
text: The Russian Ground Forces received upgraded BMPT Terminator 2 fire support vehicles with improved sighting systems and electronic warfare capabilities, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The Terminator, designed to protect tanks in urban environments, is equipped with 2A42 autocannons and Ataka anti-tank guided missiles. The 1st Guards Tank Army's maintenance battalions implemented the modifications in field conditions. Ukrainian NLAW and Javelin teams had previously targeted Terminator vehicles in Mariupol. The upgraded systems include Kaktus modular armor and improved millimeter-wave detection for FPV drone threats. The 90th Guards Tank Division was among the first units to receive the upgraded vehicles. Test operations near Kreminna demonstrated improved survivability in dense urban terrain.

title: Russian S-400 system downs NATO reconnaissance aircraft
text: Russian S-400 Triumf air defense systems of the 4th Air Defense Division engaged and destroyed a NATO RQ-4B Global Hawk strategic reconnaissance drone over the Black Sea, the Russian Ministry of Defense announced. The drone had been conducting intelligence collection operations within Russia's claimed Air Defense Identification Zone. The engagement was coordinated with the Black Sea Fleet's radar network. NATO condemned the action, noting the drone was in international airspace. The Russian Aerospace Defense Forces' 1st Special Purpose Air Defense Army maintained elevated readiness. Russia's MiG-31BM interceptors had shadowed the drone for over two hours before engagement authorization was granted. The incident heightened tensions between Russia and NATO.

title: Russian Marine Infantry captures riverine positions in Kherson
text: Russian 810th Naval Infantry Brigade units conducted amphibious landing operations on Kherson region river islands, establishing new observation posts within range of the Ukrainian-held city. Ukrainian 28th Mechanized Brigade and 126th Territorial Defense Brigade units attempted counterattacks. Russian Grad-KU rocket artillery on riverboats provided fire support. Orlan-10 drones conducted round-the-clock surveillance of Ukrainian river crossing preparations. Ukrainian Neptune missiles remained on alert, as did Russian Bastion P coastal defense complexes. The Russian Southern Military District coordinated the operation. Mine-clearing operations using robotic Uran-6 systems preceded the amphibious assault. The Russian National Guard conducted security operations in the recently captured positions.

title: ATACMS strike on Russian airbase in Crimea partially unsuccessful
text: A Ukrainian ATACMS strike on Saki airbase in Crimea was partially intercepted by Russian Pantsir-S1 and S-400 Triumf air defense systems operated by the 31st Air Defense Division. Several missiles reached the airfield, damaging aircraft infrastructure. Russian Su-24M bombers of the 43rd Naval Assault Aviation Regiment suffered minor damage. The Russian Black Sea Fleet Command intensified air defense procedures. Russia's 126th Separate Guards Reconnaissance Brigade monitored Ukrainian HIMARS launcher positions and conducted retaliatory strikes. The Russian Aerospace Forces subsequently conducted extensive Kh-101 cruise missile strikes on Ukrainian airfields used to support ATACMS operations. Ukrainian defenders had employed A-22 Fox aircraft to decoy Russian air defense systems prior to the missile launch.

title: Russia deploys new Malva wheeled howitzer to frontlines
text: The Russian Ground Forces deployed 2S43 Malva 152mm wheeled self-propelled howitzers to the Zaporizhzhia front, the Russian Ministry of Defense confirmed. The Malva, built on a MZKT-6922 8x8 chassis, offers greater strategic mobility than tracked systems like the 2S19 Msta-S. Units of the 58th Combined Arms Army received the first batch of 12 howitzers. The systems will supplement the existing Msta-S and 2S35 Koalitsiya-SV artillery park. Ammunition logistics were adapted to handle the standard 152mm rounds used by multiple Russian artillery types. Ukrainian 45th Artillery Brigade positions were targeted in the first Malva combat employment. Russian 3M60 Kalibr-class precision rounds were tested in combination with Krasnopol laser-guided shells for maximum effect.

title: Russian Combined Arms Army operations in Toretsk sector
text: The Russian 41st Combined Arms Army and 2nd Army Corps launched coordinated assaults in the Toretsk sector, advancing through the northern industrial zones. The 74th Motorized Rifle Brigade spearheaded the attack with T-72B3M main battle tanks, supported by BMP-3 infantry fighting vehicles of the 68th Army Corps. Ukrainian 24th Mechanized Brigade and the 56th Motorized Infantry Brigade conducted mobile defense operations, employing Leopard 1A5 tanks and M2A2 Bradley IFVs. Russian Sukhoi Su-34 aircraft dropped 1.5-ton FAB-1500 M-54 glide bombs on Ukrainian strongpoints. FPV drones from both sides contested every street. Russian Tornado-G systems suppressed Ukrainian artillery. The General Staff described the Toretsk operation as a major advance in the overall operational concept.

title: Russian Naval Infantry conducts training exercise in Pacific
text: The Russian Pacific Fleet's 40th Naval Infantry Brigade and 155th Guards Naval Infantry Brigade conducted large-scale amphibious assault exercises on the Kamchatka Peninsula. The exercise involved Ropucha-class landing ships delivering tanks and armored vehicles ashore. Ka-29 shipborne assault helicopters provided air assault capability. Su-25SM3 close air support aircraft from the 18th Guards Ground Attack Aviation Regiment provided simulated fire support. S-300V4 systems were deployed to practice integrated air defense during amphibious operations. Tor-M2DT systems designed for Arctic operations participated. The commander of the Pacific Fleet, Admiral Arkady Romanov, supervised the exercise. The drills were described as preparation for diverse contingencies in the Asia-Pacific region.

title: Russia's long-range artillery devastates Ukrainian supply lines
text: Russian 2S7M Malka 203mm self-propelled howitzers and BM-30 Smerch multiple rocket launchers disrupted Ukrainian supply lines in the Donetsk direction, the Russian Ministry of Defense reported. Ammunition convoys of the Ukrainian 10th Army Corps were struck at multiple points simultaneously, destroying stockpiles of 155mm artillery rounds supplied by NATO. A Ukrainian 19th Missile Brigade's ammunition depot was hit by Iskander-M ballistic missiles. The Russian 49th Combined Arms Army coordinated the interdiction campaign over 72 hours. Reconnaissance data from Orion drones identified the convoy routes. Ukraine's M270 MLRS systems returned fire, targeting Russian logistics hubs. The Russian 1st Guards Tank Army repositioned to take advantage of the weakened supply situation.

title: S-350E Poliment-Redut systems bolster Russian air defenses
text: The Russian Aerospace Defense Forces deployed additional S-350E Poliment-Redut medium-range air defense missile systems to strengthen the air defense network in the Belgorod and Kursk regions, the Russian Ministry of Defense stated. The systems, operated by the 4th Air Defense Division, complement the S-400 Triumf and S-300V4 long-range systems already in the region. The Poliment-Redut can engage up to 16 targets simultaneously with 9M96E2 missiles. Ukrainian ATACMS and Storm Shadow cruise missile attacks prompted the reinforcement. The 48th Air Defense Corps coordinated the deployment. Pantsir-S2 systems provide short-range coverage of the Poliment-Redut batteries. The Russian 6th Air Army's radar coverage has been significantly extended by the new deployments.

title: Russian armored forces regroup for offensive in Sumy direction
text: Russian armored forces, including the 2nd Guards Combined Arms Army and elements of the 1st Guards Tank Army, are regrouping for potential offensive operations in the Sumy direction, Russian military sources indicated. The 4th Guards Tank Division and 200th Separate Motor Rifle Brigade completed redeployment from other sectors. Ukrainian 27th Rocket Artillery Brigade tracked Russian logistics movements using M17 UAVs. Russian 2S35 Koalitsiya-SV and 2S7M Malka howitzers were moved forward. T-90M Proryv tanks equipped with Shtora-1 active defense systems lead armored elements. Russian Mi-28NM Night Hunter helicopters provide round-the-clock reconnaissance and fire support. The General Staff of the Russian Armed Forces denied offensive intentions, describing movements as routine rotation.
